body.title: [sprint] Retire nomic vectors entirely — redefine all consumers in node-overlap, delete embedder.py + cache + PG table
body.intention: I intend that no dense-vector embedding survives — every embedding use in igor is node-overlap, one coherent shape.
body.description:
**Problem:** Akien: retire nomic entirely; all uses -> graph-tree. The cosine-shaped consumers must be redefined and the nomic machinery deleted.
**Proposed shape:** ONE replacement pass over ALL embedding consumers, BOTH stacks — nothing dense survives. Stack A (Igor live, nomic): judgments.py valence anchors, search-rerank, factual_compression, distillation, narrative_engine, cortex attractor-adoption + stored-vector compares. Stack B (Scraps/librarian + devlab): scraps/embedding_engine.py, librarian recall.py/memory_writer.py/embedding_drain.py, devlab competition kNN, tools/notebook.py. Redefine each in node-overlap terms, then DELETE embedder.py (nomic/Ollama) AND scraps/embedding_engine.py (OpenAI/hash), both file caches (~/.unseen_university/cache/embeddings/), and BOTH memory_embeddings tables (clan + competition). SUPERSEDES T-igor-embedder-via-rack.
**NO BACKFILL NEEDED:** Igor is starting over — all old memories killed — so there is no corpus to strand; retire-nomic has NO backfill prerequisite (T-graph-embed-backfill-existing closed as moot).
**Affected files:** cognition/judgments.py, narrative_engine.py, factual_compression.py, distillation.py, memory/cortex.py, cognition/embedder.py (delete), scraps/embedding_engine.py (delete), librarian/recall.py, librarian/memory_writer.py, librarian/embedding_drain.py, scraps/scraps_device.py, igor/tools/notebook.py, devlab competition kNN.
**Design rules:** no dense vectors remain anywhere; no SQLite; log the schema/table drops.
**Scope boundary:** redefine ALL consumers (both stacks) + delete both nomic AND OpenAI/hash embedders + drop both tables; gated only on the node-overlap LIKE path existing (memory-and-like).
**Completion criteria:** grep embedder.py / embedding_engine.py / nomic / memory_embeddings -> 0 live refs repo-wide; every former cosine consumer (both stacks) now uses node-overlap; igor + librarian + scraps import green and their tests green.
**Test plan:** grep-gate red->green on nomic/embedder.py/memory_embeddings; per-consumer test that valence/kNN/dedup still function via node-overlap.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
